class.name: WrapAnchor
class.brief_description: A [RopeAnchor] that wraps a rope around geometry by ray-casting a helical sweep onto nearby surfaces.
class.description: [WrapAnchor] generates anchor positions by sweeping a helix around the local +X axis at [member wrap_radius] and casting a ray from each helix point radially inward toward the X axis. Each ray that hits a physics body produces a contact point. The result is a series of local-space positions that conform to whatever geometry is inside the sweep. Two budget parameters control how many positions are produced. [member max_turns] limits the helix length. [member wrap_length] limits the accumulated real rope length in meters. Generation stops as soon as either budget is exhausted. The axial pitch per turn is [member rope_width] plus [member turn_gap]. Setting [member unevenness] above [code]0.0[/code] blends the linear pitch toward a sine-wave profile, which creates an uneven, organic wrapping pattern. [b]Note:[/b] [member particles_per_meter] should match [member Rope.particles_per_meter] on the rope referencing this anchor to keep particle spacing consistent.

members.member: When [code]true[/code], the helix winds clockwise around the local +X axis when viewed from the positive X direction. When [code]false[/code], it winds counter-clockwise.
Physics layers to ray-cast against when rebuilding positions. Only bodies on matching layers are considered. Set to [code]0[/code] to disable collision and produce no positions.
When [code]true[/code], draws the cached contact positions and the inward rays each frame using DebugDraw3D. Automatically enables per-frame processing while active.
Maximum number of full helix turns in the angular sweep. Acts as an upper bound on how far around the +X axis the sweep travels. Generation also stops early if [member wrap_length] is reached first.
Starting angle of the helix sweep, in degrees. Rotates the entire wrapped path around the +X axis without changing the number of turns or the arc length.

Number of ray-cast sample points tested per meter of helix circumference. Higher values reduce the chance of missing narrow geometry but increase the cost of each rebuild. Should match [member Rope.particles_per_meter] of the rope referencing this anchor.

Width of the rope in meters. Determines the axial pitch between successive turns and the outward offset applied to each contact point along the surface normal. Should match [member Rope.rope_width] of the rope referencing this anchor.

Extra axial gap in meters added between each full turn, on top of [member rope_width]. Positive values space turns further apart; negative values compress them. The total axial pitch per turn is [code]rope_width + turn_gap[/code].

Blends the axial pitch between a uniform linear profile ([code]0.0[/code]) and a sine-wave profile ([code]1.0[/code]). At [code]1.0[/code], contact points bunch toward the ends of the sweep and spread apart in the middle, simulating an organic or hand-wound rope.

Maximum total surface arc distance in meters. Generation stops once the accumulated real distance between accepted contact points reaches this value, or when [member max_turns] is exhausted, whichever comes first.

Radius of the helix sweep from the local +X axis, in meters. Each ray is cast from this distance inward toward the axis. The radius should be large enough that the helix origin lies outside the geometry being wrapped.
